architecture_fit.note: No transformer at all — reads ground-truth parent_ids and writes one-hot logits; a huge departure from base_model.py with no QK mechanism.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on benchmark's built-in uniform baseline constant; no strawman/no-circuit ablation run or shown by the attempt itself.

faithfulness.note: Circular — model_fn re-derives the exact target from structural labels (the answer key); no causal check, no model, and the README doesn't acknowledge this.

operating_range.note: Sweeps depths 2/3/4 and four path rules, but only ~2x node-count range and all trivially 1.0 since it reads the answer, so it can never break.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-built and on GPU, but encodes the answer as logits rather than expressing a genuine Q/K attention circuit, so it shows little mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is plain text boxes with ASCII bars (all full at 1.0) — not the heatmap/query-slider/red-✕ the README claims.

visualisation_rationale.note: Well-written rationale describes a heatmap with axes and markers that the actual app.py does not implement.

notes: The attempt scores a perfect 1.0 on every metric but achieves it by reading the ground-truth tree structure and writing one-hot attention directly at the computed target — an answer-key lookup, not an attention circuit, and a wholesale departure from base_model.py. It provides no causal evidence, no genuine baseline contrast, and the Gradio Demo tab is text boxes rather than the heatmap its README describes. Mean of scored items lands at borderline; the headline metric is real but mechanistically empty.
